# Rare Word Alternatives — review file

*Generated by `scripts/18_rare_word_alternatives.py` — do not hand-edit.*

All 3460 remaining KJV words with 1–2 uses bible-wide (non-proper-noun, currently verdict `period`). For each: the KJV's own renderings of the same Hebrew/Greek word elsewhere (allowed use of the KJV — similar-word lookup, not authenticity evidence), and the words other bibles use at the same verses that the KJV does not.

- **KJV renders the same original word elsewhere as:** humble (5), abased (4), humbleth (2), yourselves (2), brought (1), low (1), humbled (1)

- **I Samuel 4:5** — And when the ark of the covenant of the Lord came into the camp, all Israel shouted with a great shout, so that the earth rang again.

  - Wycliffe uses: al, arke, boond, castels, come, criede
  - Webster uses: resounded
  - YLT uses: at, cometh, coming, in, is, it
  - DRC uses: come, was
- **I Kings 1:45** — And Zadok the priest and Nathan the prophet have anointed him king in Gihon: and they are come up from thence rejoicing, so that the city rang again. This is the noise that ye have heard.
  - Geneva1599 uses: citie, gone, haue, ioy, moued, vp
  - Wycliffe uses: anoyntiden, beynge, camen, citee, doun, fro
  - Webster uses: resounded

- **Isaiah 44:5** — One shall say, I am the Lord’s; and another shall call himself by the name of Jacob; and another shall subscribe with his hand unto the Lord, and surname himself by the name of Israel.
  - Geneva1599 uses: called, himselfe, iaakob, lordes, shalbe, vnto
  - Wycliffe uses: be, clepe, he, hoond, in, licned
  - Webster uses: to
  - YLT uses: calleth, fi, for, jehovah, saith, surnameth
  - DRC uses: to
- **Jeremiah 32:44** — Men shall buy fields for money, and subscribe evidences, and seal them, and take witnesses in the land of Benjamin, and in the places about Jerusalem, and in the cities of Judah, and in the cities of the mountains, and in the cities of the valley, and in the cities of the south: for I will cause their captivity to return, saith the Lord. 
  - Geneva1599 uses: beniamin, captiuitie, ierusalem, iudah, make, mountaines
  - Wycliffe uses: a, at, be, ben, beniamyn, book
  - YLT uses: a, affirmation, an, as, back, book
  - DRC uses: are, back, be, bought, bring, deeds
